insights.title: Lending Market Concentration Risk
insights.description: The top lender (Bond investors) accounts for 16.7% of total lending volume (€1270m of €7586m). Top 5 lenders control 44% of deal flow, indicating significant concentration risk.

insights.title: Media-Lending Sector Divergence
insights.description: Sectors trending in industry media (Investment, General, Commercial) are underrepresented in current lending activity. This suggests either emerging opportunity or media hype yet to translate into deal flow.

insights.title: US Residential Pricing Intelligence
insights.description: Across 70 residential data points, average sale price is $231k with Zillow listings averaging $141/sqft. Properties built after 2000 command a 23% premium over older stock, mirroring the 'flight to quality' seen in European CRE markets.

insights.title: Public vs Private CRE Capital Imbalance
insights.description: Listed REITs in our dataset represent $536B in market cap while sampled private lending deals total €7.6B. The 'wall of capital' in public markets suggests significant dry powder for acquisitions, which could drive cap rate compression when deployed.

insights.title: Geographic Intelligence: CRE Hotspot Analysis
insights.description: Across all data sources, London appears most frequently (23 mentions), followed by Germany (12), Manchester (11), ZIP 32312 (6), ZIP 32309 (5). Cross-referencing news sentiment with lending activity reveals these as the most active CRE markets.

insights.title: Property Type Intelligence
insights.description: 'Residential' is the most referenced property type across all sources with 84 mentions. The data shows convergence between media coverage, lending activity and REIT focus towards Residential, Office, Hotel assets.
